one_line_ask: Take a batch of inconsistent distributor-supplied product photos for a firearms/optics/outdoor Shopify store and turn them into clean, straightened, color-corrected, pure-white-background, square marketplace hero images (plus transparent-PNG tile variants and a vectorized listing badge), all organized into a review board and a tidy asset folder.
brief:
I run a hands-off Shopify store selling thermal cameras, night-vision optics, firearms, ammunition, trail-camera mounts and hunting accessories. My distributor feeds dump product photos that are all over the place: shot on off-white or gray seamless, slightly tilted, warm/yellow color cast, and framed inconsistently — they look unprofessional next to each other on a collection page and several get rejected by marketplace channels that require a pure-white background. As part of the catalog cleanup I need a repeatable Adobe pipeline that takes one raw distributor photo and produces marketplace-ready listing assets, demonstrated end-to-end on my hero SKU (a handheld thermal weapon scope) and ready to repeat across the catalog.

For the hero SKU, starting from the single raw distributor JPEG I provide, deliver: (1) a corrected master — auto-straightened, cropped to the product, exposure/tone normalized, and the warm color cast neutralized; (2) a pure #FFFFFF background version where the scope is isolated and the messy seamless is replaced with solid white per Shopify/Amazon hero spec; (3) a transparent-PNG cut-out of the same scope for collection tiles and bundle mock-ups; (4) a squar
